zotero.abstractNote: Foundation models are typically trained on massive datasets using self-supervised learning. While they often exhibit similar emergent capabilities, they differ significantly in their architecture, training objectives, and data curation. In this work, we provide a systematic framework to understand and quantify these differences. We introduce a set of metrics based on the geometry of the learned representations and the dynamics of the training process. We apply our framework to a suite of state-of-the-art foundation models, revealing distinct clusters that correspond to different design choices. Our findings provide insights into the trade-offs between different pre-training paradigms and offer a principled way to select models for downstream tasks.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems
